git_releases.summary: Wan2.2 Vast workflow machine-registry production path

runtime_strategies.description: Use the base Vast Comfy image and do not treat known machines as cache.
Prefer previously successful machines and enable WarmStart only when one is selected.
Use a project Docker environment image through a Vast template to preinstall KJ custom nodes and Python dependencies. Model weights are still downloaded or cache-probed at runtime.

workflow_branches.notes: Single-segment direct output path. This is the current stable production branch.

Segmented generation path. v3 single-instance remains the default candidate after verified 30s and 60s Vast runs. v4 anchor-overlap produced real 30s output after manual rescue but failed visual frame review because later segments re-generated different composition and identity; do not use v4 for 30s/60s production.

workflow_branches.source_workflow: workflows/书墨-30s长视频-wan2-2AnimateKJ版_v2版-参考动作、表情.json
workflow_branches.notes: 2.0 KJ single-shot 30s branch. It uses a pure-color transparent IP image plus a 30s reference video, redraws the background from prompt, and avoids segmented stitching. Initial 30s validation succeeded, but cold-start and inference cost are high, so this is a candidate branch rather than the production default.

workflow_branches.source_workflow: workflows/书墨-30s长视频-wan2-2AnimateKJ版_v2版-参考动作、表情.json
workflow_branches.notes: Experimental KJ long-duration wrapper. It splits input video into 30s chunks, runs the existing KJ 30s workflow sequentially on one Vast instance, downloads each segment, and merges with local ffmpeg concat. It does not add a ComfyUI merge plugin and does not provide continue_motion continuity because this KJ workflow has no continue_motion input.

implementation_variants.description: Project-owned KJ environment image and Vast template path. The image starts from vastai/comfy:v0.19.3-cuda-12.9-py312, preinstalls KJ custom nodes, Python dependencies, and records the torch/torchvision/torchaudio compatibility state, while deliberately excluding Wan/KJ model weights. Runtime strategy 1.2-docker-env-template uses the resulting Vast template hash while preserving R2 staging, onstart, HF speed gate, machine registry, and normal download/publish flow. The default registry is GHCR so the build path does not depend on a DockerHub username.

torch_policy.core_check: torch CUDA runtime >= 12.4 and at least one CUDA device
torch_policy.auxiliary_install_policy: install missing/import-broken auxiliary packages with --no-deps matched to current torch version; do not force reinstall torch

implementation_variants.description: Single-shot KJ 30s implementation. Stages a fixed transparent-background IP image and a 30s reference video, converts the KJ canvas workflow to API JSON, disables torch compile on RTX 3090, runs one 481-frame 720x720 prompt, downloads the real VHS output node, and publishes the result. It now runs a HuggingFace speed preflight before bootstrap and includes a 4090 candidate selector that lists 10-20 offers, short-rents 1-3 per batch, and continues to the next batch when none pass.

validated_runs.notes: Generation succeeded after disabling node 137 WanVideoTorchCompileSettings and removing WanVideoModelLoader.compile_args. The output was downloaded and manually published because the first run hit a Windows GBK vastai logs encoding issue during timing summarization; the runner was patched afterward.

implementation_variants.description: Splits videos longer than 30s into 30s chunks, stages per-segment KJ runtime workflows, runs them sequentially on one Vast instance via the existing KJ remote submit script, then downloads and ffmpeg-concats the segment outputs. The current accepted fixed-scene 2.0 path is B1.1 same-frame anchor: reuse one complete person+background anchor image as ip_image.png for every 30s segment and leave WanVideoAnimateEmbeds without bg_images or mask. The separate B2 bg_images/mask experiment is retained as a failed record because it suppressed mouth/body motion. The branch also treats reference-video overlays as a probabilistic quality risk and uses preflight reference-overlay scanning plus postflight generated-artifact review before accepting long-video outputs. Cleanup roadmap: 2.0 keeps rule-based detection and targeted local cleaning; 2.1 local rules and 2.1-next OCR/LaMa samples failed on the current heavily overlaid source; next work should use clean source footage, manual cleanup, or stronger semantic/video object removal such as ProPainter/E2FGVI before any Vast inference.

b2_background_conditioning.ip_image: 素材资产/美女图无背景纯色/纯色坐着.png
b2_background_conditioning.mask: LoadImageMask alpha from ip_image.png -> InvertMask -> GrowMask
b2_background_conditioning.failure_reason: bg_images plus mask over-constrained the KJ animate embedding path, and the generated background image contained the full person, suppressing mouth/body motion.

reference_overlay_cleaning.gate: Run analyzer before and after cleaning, inspect before/after contact sheet, and do not enter Vast stage/inference unless both human review and risk-after pass.

latest_local_validation.notes: The local rule cleaner removed or muted some safe upper overlays and small tokens, but near-body speech bubbles, mid-body text, and bottom subtitles remained visible or became gray blocks. This is not a valid reference for paid KJ inference.

tested_methods: thicker OCR masks and local mask variants
latest_next_validation.notes: OCR detects the Chinese overlays, but local inpaint leaves visible text, outlines, white/orange/gray blocks, or bubble frames. LaMa removes some overlays but hallucinates photovoltaic panels or semi-transparent body/clothing where text and bubbles overlap the person. Do not use this cleaned reference for KJ inference.

generated_artifact_polishing.purpose: After a merged KJ output is downloaded, run local generated-video risk detection, repair isolated colored dots or sticker specks with OpenCV inpaint, rewrap audio with FFmpeg, then run after-scan and generate before/after review sheets.
default_safety: pad each detector target by two frames to cover sub-second edge misses
attempt local silver/white halo cleanup around confirmed targets
keep cyan/blue off by default in photovoltaic scenes to avoid sky and panel false positives
not_for: large overlays that polluted pose conditioning

latest_validation.notes: v5 generalizes the repair path from red-only to detector-reported red/yellow/green/magenta candidates and adds a local silver/white halo residual mask around confirmed targets. On this sample, the safe auto-repair target remains the red hanging-ball artifact; cyan/blue stayed disabled to avoid the sky and photovoltaic panel background. A 48px halo probe removed more string/highlight residue but caused obvious blue-gray smearing on the leg edge and panel background, so it is not the default. User accepted the v5 output as good enough for now and it is the recommended polished output for this run.

validated_runs.notes: B1.1 reuses one complete person+photovoltaic-background anchor frame as ip_image.png for both 30s segments and keeps the original KJ node 171 structure without bg_images or mask. The 5s+5s smoke test restored motion, and the full 30s+30s test passed keyframes, seam, 26-30s, 48-52s, and generated-artifact review. This is the current accepted 2.0 fixed-scene path.

B2 reused the same pure seated IP image, generated bg_image.png, prompt/negative/seed, and IP-alpha-derived mask for both 30s segments. A quick structural review did not find double head or duplicate person, but detailed comparison showed mouth and body motion were nearly locked. The bg_images/mask patch over-constrained node 171, and bg_image.png contained a complete person rather than true background-only content. Do not use this B2 structure for the current KJ 2.0 path.

Initial two-segment output technically merged but failed visual review because segment 2 hallucinated a standing duplicate/background person. The live instance was kept and only segment 2 was rerun with a new seed and stronger single-seated-person prompt/negative prompt. The s01 + s02fix merge removed the duplicate person, but later review found segment 1 and segment 2 did not keep a consistent chair state, so this run is not accepted.

Two 30s segments reused the same fixed photovoltaic background prompt and the seated pure-color IP image. Quick review showed stable background and no duplicate body, but detailed review found hand-motion drift around final 49.5s-50.5s. The corresponding reference window is segment 2 19.5s-20.5s, where large sticker/location text covers the lower body and hand area. Conclusion: overlay text can contaminate KJ action/pose conditioning even when the final output does not redraw the text; clean the reference video before rerunning.

The run reused the previously accepted first 30s segment and reran only segment 2 after cleaning the 49.3s-52.0s hand-anchor overlay window in the 60s reference. The prior hand/multi-hand drift around final 49.5s-50.5s improved, but detailed review found a short-lived red point around final 29.6s near the right leg/chair area. Corresponding reference frames around 26s-30s contain red UI tokens such as checkmarks and a location bubble. Conclusion: overlay contamination is probabilistic and can manifest as extra hands, hand drift, red dots, or sticker residue; acceptances require both preflight reference-overlay review and postflight generated-artifact review.

implementation_variants.description: Split video into 10s segments, generate independently, then stitch with ffmpeg.
Adds tail-frame / continue-motion support on top of segmented v1.

Single-instance segmented implementation. Splits source video into 10s segments, carries 5 tail frames forward with continue_motion, downloads all segment outputs, and ffmpeg-concats the merged result.

Failed visual experiment. Anchors every 10s segment on the original reference image, cuts later segments with an overlap head, and merges downloaded outputs locally with ffmpeg xfade/acrossfade. It avoided v3 continue_motion identity carryover but broke cross-segment continuity because each segment re-generated a different composition.

validation_runs.notes: Initial remote submission executed only temp preview node 299 because v4 removed the required continue_motion_max_frames input. The live instance was rescued by resubmitting patched prompts that kept continue_motion_max_frames=5, removed non-save VHS preview outputs, and executed save_output node 341. The script was then patched and PrepareOnly verified. Later frame review showed the back half is unusable: s02 and s03 already re-generate the person, pose, chair, and framing differently before merge, so xfade/acrossfade cannot repair continuity.
